programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A visit to his convalescent aunt, prompts Barnaby to investigate a series of suspicious deaths at the nursing home where she is staying.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a local thief and womanizer is killed with a pitchfork, the residents of Midsomer Mallow fear for their chances in the Perfect Village Competition.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Mysterious events and discovery of his relative's body lead the resident to believe that Jonathan Lowrie's ghost is in their community.

programme.title: Magnum P.I.
programme.desc: Magnum takes a case from fellow private investigator Luther Gillis to find the true identity of a man's comatose fiancée when he learns that she's had extensive facial surgery.

programme.title: Magnum P.I.
programme.desc: An art connoisseur and close friend of Higgins who recently hired Magnum to test his security is murdered, and Thomas is arrested as the main suspect.

programme.title: Stir of Echoes
programme.desc: After being hypnotized during a party, a man begins seeing visions involving the ghost of a young woman.
A man who specialises in debunking paranormal occurrences checks into fabled room 1408 in the Dolphin Hotel.

programme.title: Winchester
programme.desc: A widow haunted. A house possessed. A family cursed. Step inside the most haunted house in history.

programme.title: Dark Skies
programme.desc: The Barret family comes to learn that a terrifying and deadly force is after them.

programme.title: Blackwater Lane
programme.desc: A woman drives by a stranded motorist who is has been murdered. The woman believes she is the next victim.

programme.title: Eye See You
programme.desc: Recovering from the psychological effects of witnessing a brutal crime, FBI Agent Jake Malloy (Sylvester Stallone) checks into a rehabilitation clinic for law enforcement officers in the remote wilderness of Wyoming during the dead of winter. He soon finds himself helping other patients as a serial killer begins killing them one by one.

programme.title: Killing Season
programme.desc: Oscar winner Robert De Niro ("Raging Bull") and Oscar nominee John Travolta ("Pulp Fiction") star as two Bosnian War veterans hunting each other in this thrilling game of cat and mouse.

programme.title: The Contract
programme.desc: Frank Carden (Morgan Freeman), one of the world's greatest assassins, is handed a lucrative contract to kill a highly secretive billionaire. But in a twist of fate, Carden is involved in a car accident during the job, and when his gun is found after he is admitted to a hospital, federal marshals arrest him. Meanwhile, ex-cop Ray Keene (John Cusack) is hiking with his son, Chris (Jamie Anderson), when Carden's cohorts try to spring him from custody, and soon the pair is caught in the crossfire.

programme.title: Animal Factory [Broadcast Edit]
programme.desc: Suburbanite Ron (Edward Furlong) is spoiled, young and not overly worried about the marijuana charges leveled against him. But, after being made out to be a drug dealer, he faces a five-year jail sentence in San Quentin State Prison. Physically frail and unaccustomed to his rough surroundings, Ron is primed to fall victim to sexual predators and bullying guards -- that is, until he's befriended by Earl (Willem Dafoe), a veteran inmate who finds meaning in protecting the vulnerable new kid.

programme.title: The Dark Crystal
programme.desc: A Gelfling named Jen who sets out to restore balance to his world by finding a missing shard of a magical crystal

programme.title: Monty Python and the Holy Grail
programme.desc: King Arthur and his Knights of the Round Table embark on a surreal.

programme.title: Man Of The Year
programme.desc: A comedian who hosts a news satire program decides to run for president, and a computerized voting machine malfunction gets him elected.

programme.title: The Loft
programme.desc: Five men share a loft for affairs; a woman’s body changes everything.

programme.title: The China Syndrome
programme.desc: A reporter finds what appears to be a cover-up of safety hazards at a nuclear power plant.

programme.title: Family Business
programme.desc: A proud tradition has been carried on for three generations: they are the best at what they do. Edited for TV

programme.title: Whatever It Takes
programme.desc: A nerdy high school senior and the class jock conspire to help each other win the hearts of their dream girls.

programme.title: Running With Scissors
programme.desc: An adult man looks back on his childhood with his bipolar and self-centered mother.

programme.title: A Woman Of Distinction
programme.desc: A publicist's efforts to create a buzz about a British astronomer's visit create personal and professional problems for a career-minded dean of a women's college.

programme.title: Twentieth Century
programme.desc: Carole Lombard and John Barrymore star in this all-time classic screwball comedy based on the Charles MacArthur-Ben Hecht Broadway hit and directed by Howard Hawks. It s the story of a maniacal Broadway director (Barrymore) who transforms shopgirl Carole Lombard from a talented amateur to a smashing Great White Way success adored by public and press.

programme.title: You Can't Take It With You
programme.desc: Lionel Barrymore is the eccentric patriarch of a clan of frustrated artists who decided 30 years earlier to retire from the rat race and use his fortune to encourage friends and family to pursue vocations that really interest them. At the center of his family is his granddaughter, Jean Arthur, who is carrying on a romance with her boss son, James Stewart.

programme.title: Holiday
programme.desc: A fun-loving, self-made man forgoes marrying a straight-laced heiress for her black sheep sister.

programme.title: Journey to the End of Night
programme.desc: A son and his father separately plot to escape their lives in the underworld of Brazil's sex industry.

programme.title: Cobra Verde
programme.desc: The feared bandit Cobra Verde (Klaus Kinski) is hired by a plantation owner to supervise his slaves. After the owner suspects Cobra Verde of consorting with his young daughters, the owner wishes him gone. Rather than kill him,the owner sends Cobra Verde to Africa. The only white man in the area, Cobra Verde finds himself the victim of torture and humiliation. Later, he trains soldiers in a rebel army. Far from home, Cobra Verde is on the edge of madness.

programme.title: The Real Blonde
programme.desc: Joe and Mary have been living together in Manhattan for six years. Joe is an actor, who has no agent and no thesping credits, but whose ambitions are very high. He works as a waiter at a cafe. Mary works as a make-up stylist for hot fashion photographer Blair, and she pays most of the pair's bills. Joe finally lowers his standards and accepts a degrading bit in a Madonna video, while his friend and co-waiter Bob gets a high-paying job on a soap opera opposite siren Kelly.

programme.title: If… Dog… Rabbit
programme.desc: Johnnie Cooper (Matthew Modine) served time for an armed robbery, in which his father and brother also participated. When he's released from jail, he's determined to live a normal life, and gets work at a gas station. But soon his brother pays a visit.

programme.title: Now You See Me 2
programme.desc: The Four Horsemen resurface, and are recruited by a tech genius to pull off their most impossible heist yet.

programme.title: Empire State
programme.desc: Two childhood friends plan to rob an armored-car depository, but an NYPD officer stands in their way.

programme.title: Force of Nature
programme.desc: A gang of thieves plan a heist during a hurricane, but a cop tries to force everyone inside to evacuate.

programme.title: First Kill
programme.desc: A young boy is kidnapped, and his father must locate a cache of stolen money to save him.

programme.title: Lone Star Shark 2
programme.desc: A new victim has been chosen to face off against a ravenous shark in a Texas lake.

programme.title: Pinkerton
programme.desc: Allan Pinkerton, founder of America's first detective agency, embarks on a mission to capture the notorious outlaw, Jesse James.

programme.title: The Wide West
programme.desc: 1907. A mysterious gambler aids a couple of Japanese sumo wrestlers, the local Judge and a beautiful store owner against ruthless outlaws who have attacked a small Californian town.

programme.title: A Witness to Murder
programme.desc: Sanitation worker MaryBeth is in the wrong place at the wrong time when the mayor's wife is murdered. She refuses to talk to the sheriff she dislikes, but the killers know she was there. Now they're coming for her to make sure she stays silent.

programme.title: 12 To Midnight
programme.desc: A detective gets kicked off the force when the murder of his wife drives him to drink. When a new string of murders seem connected to her death, he soon finds himself squaring off against a killer who appears to be superhuman.

programme.title: Pinkerton
programme.desc: Allan Pinkerton, founder of America's first detective agency, embarks on a mission to capture the notorious outlaw, Jesse James.

programme.title: Hansan: Rising Dragon
programme.desc: In 1592, Korea faces the threat of the powerful Japanese navy. As defending Adm. Yi Sun-sin's forces fall into crisis, they resort to a secret weapon: the dragon-headed ships known as geobukseon. Using them, they hope to turn the tide of the war.

programme.title: Rise of the Legend
programme.desc: A powerful martial arts master (Eddie Peng) returns to the town where his father was murdered to take on a ruthless crime boss (Sammo Hung).

programme.title: Kung Fu League
programme.desc: When Fei Ying's manager stops him from getting the girl he loves, he magically summons help from kung fu legends.

programme.title: Striking Rescue
programme.desc: A veteran Muay Thai expert goes on a take-no-prisoners mission of revenge after his wife and daughter are brutally murdered by mysterious forces.

programme.title: Deceitful Dating
programme.desc: Is Alice, the new neighbor now dating John, as sweet as she seems?

programme.title: Beyond the Headlines: The Tiffany Rubin Story
programme.desc: It was every parent's nightmare.

programme.title: My Daughter Must Live
programme.desc: Meghan O'Malley learns her husband is not the girl's biological father.

programme.title: A Nurse To Die For
programme.desc: A father hires a live-in nurse to help care for his sick daughter.

programme.title: Deceitful Dating
programme.desc: Is Alice, the new neighbor now dating John, as sweet as she seems?

programme.title: Falling For the Competition
programme.desc: A local mom’s and dad’s group clash for the prestigious Modern Parenting Group of the Year award.

programme.title: Candid About Love
programme.desc: Laura must get reclusive artist Shane to premiere his work at a gallery to help her own career.

programme.title: Buried Secrets
programme.desc: A cop-turned-writer creates suspicion when she publishes her first novel.

programme.title: A Match for the Prince
programme.desc: A matchmaker risks falling for a real-life prince before his royal wedding date.

programme.title: Been There All Along
programme.desc: Charley, the founder of a dating app must team up with marketing expert Liam, to save her company.

programme.title: Take Back
programme.desc: Zara (Gillian White) and Brian (Michael Jai White) are living the perfect small town life when a mysterious stranger from Zara's secret past kidnaps their daughter and now they must race to save her life.

programme.title: Just Getting Started
programme.desc: Duke Diver is living the high life as the freewheeling manager of a luxurious resort in Palm Springs, Calif. He soon faces competition from Leo, a former military man who likes the same woman that Duke is interested in. When Diver's past suddenly catches up with him, he must put aside his differences and reluctantly team up with Leo to stop whoever is trying to kill him.

programme.title: Traffickers
programme.desc: Tiffany Cooper, disgraced by the military, wanted by the cops and running out of time, stops at nothing to take down a Trafficking Operation and save these young girls. Nothing is what it seems when everyone seems to be against you. Trust No One.

programme.title: The Climb
programme.desc: The breath-taking story of two men ... one with a courageous heart who seeks God; the other with an independent spirit who relies on his own strength.

programme.title: Murder Without Motive
programme.desc: Edmund Perry has everything going for him after he graduates with a scholarship to attend Stanford. With his dedication paying off, an encounter with a police officer senselessly ends his life.

programme.title: The Comeback
programme.desc: Jeff and Tisa Murray have decided to move their family back to their hometown of Atlanta when Tisa is offered her dream job. The dream becomes a hilarious reality when family issues, Mom's new boyfriend, and ex-lovers are waiting on them upon their return.

programme.title: The Forbidden Daughter
programme.desc: When tragedy happens at a young age Demetrius loses her way, until an angel comes to rescue her and gives her the strength to face her fears.

programme.title: Loyalty
programme.desc: When independent drug dealer, Buttons, learns his girlfriend used to be his competitor, T Bones, longtime side chick, and believes they might still be messing around, it's on. T Bones' family is strong and makes sure no one crosses them by any means.

programme.title: In This House
programme.desc: Claudia is a medical student focused on building a perfect life with her live-in boyfriend Dr. Jayson. Her grandfather’s death forces her to go back to her hometown to face her estranged family and her past. Once she arrives, she uncovers sinister secrets and will stop at nothing to break the generational curse stemming from her family’s history.

programme.title: Have You Seen My Man?
programme.desc: After a long night of partying, Niecey's man Chase is nowhere to be found. Just when things couldn’t get any worse, here comes the exes and their drama. The tables turn and the finger pointing begins, Niecy knows there's no turning back now, she has to find the truth and her man.

programme.title: Dragon Crusaders
programme.desc: Fugitive Knights Templar are cursed. They must defeat the wizard-dragon to break the spell and save the world.

programme.title: War of the Worlds
programme.desc: Earth is laid to ruin when massive walkers annihilate all in their path as an astrophysicist finds his son.

programme.title: Alien Rubicon
programme.desc: A spacecraft lands in New York and heads toward our Capital. If it arrives, it will trigger an invasion.

programme.title: End Of The World
programme.desc: As a mass of solar storms causes tsunamis, volcanoes, and flooding, a family flees to high-elevation caves.

programme.title: Alien Predator
programme.desc: A black ops team sent to investigate the crash of an unidentified aircraft is hunted by the former occupants.

programme.title: Mother's Day
programme.desc: When you go into the woods today, you're in for a big surprise. When you go into the woods today, you're not going to believe your eyes. But this ain't no Teddy Bear Picnic. Three women discover that two men are willing to do anything to impress Mother, and what impresses Mother is watching her sons commit acts of rape, torture and murder. Now these women are prisoners, lowered to the status of pawns in a game of chess waged between all three of them and two dimwits and their maniacal mommy; thus, the question becomes: can any of them escape from this game alive?

programme.title: The Toxic Avenger Part 2
programme.desc: Toxie's mean, green, and back on the screen! Melvin Junko was a nerdy 98lb weakling until he fell into a vat of toxic waste, turning him into the first ever superhuman superhero from New Jersey. This time the hideously deformed creature of superhuman size and strength takes on Tokyo. The Toxic Avenger Part II is a spectacular sequel with even more ingenious special effects, even more bizarre action, and even more unbelievably famous fight scenes. He's come a long way from Tromaville, baby!

programme.title: Cottage Country
programme.desc: Todd (Labine) wants everything to be just perfect at the family cottage where he plans to propose to Cammie (Akerman). But things go awry with the arrival of Todd's slacker brother Salinger (Petronijevic) and his free-spirited girlfriend Masha (Punch). When Todd accidentally dispatches his irksome sibling with an axe, Cammie is determined not to let murder stand in the way of their happiness.

programme.title: Mark of the Devil
programme.desc: Count Christian von Meruh is a witch hunter apprentice to Lord Cumberland. He believes strongly in his mentor and the ways of the church, but loses faith when he catches Cumberlund committing a crime. He slowly begins to see for himself that the witch trials are nothing but a scam of the church to rob people of their land, money, and other personal belongings of value and seduce beautiful women.

programme.title: Mother's Day
programme.desc: When you go into the woods today, you're in for a big surprise. When you go into the woods today, you're not going to believe your eyes. But this ain't no Teddy Bear Picnic. Three women discover that two men are willing to do anything to impress Mother, and what impresses Mother is watching her sons commit acts of rape, torture and murder. Now these women are prisoners, lowered to the status of pawns in a game of chess waged between all three of them and two dimwits and their maniacal mommy; thus, the question becomes: can any of them escape from this game alive?

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: A drain clog turns out to be more than just a little matted hair and soap scum. Then, scientists fight to contain the creature they've been studying - but is the true threat one of them?

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Simon has invented an incredible virtual reality experience that allows him to join in on his favorite films ... but what happens when the virtual reality becomes Simon's actual reality?

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Jack's mother is taken from him, but her garden leaves more to remember her by than he first thought. Then, three teens meet their favorite pop star and realize she's not at all what they imagined.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: A movie buff opens a prop museum, but a rival collector could put everything at risk. Then, Jackson is being followed by a dark force, but will his attempts to rid himself of it work?

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: An art collector could never have anticipated the ways a terrifying painting would change his life. Then, life in prison is tough, but Elmer's pet spiders always have his back.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: A siren lures a doctor into her trap to carry out a diabolical plan. Then, a teenage girl must fight off a demonic plague when she realizes her family might be infected.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Tim finds the answer to needing more hours in the day, but is it worth the cost? Then, when a small town opens a time capsule, the local librarian must try to keep history from repeating itself.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: A family is detained, and a young girl's distressing symptoms quickly escalate without her medication. Then, when the dead rise from their graves, townspeople take justice into their own hands.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: After the death of a loved one, a drinking habit becomes insatiable. Then, dolls are Evie's favorite companions, but when a severed toy head appears in her dollhouse, Evie can no longer protect them.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: When Miss Hathaway gets sick and Mr. Drysdale has to speak at a conference, Jed helps out by running the bank and having Elly be his secretary, while Jethro finds a speech that Drysdale accidentally uses instead of his own. All the while a new teller sucks-up to the men and flirts with the women.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Psychiatrist Gets Clampetted" - Jethro finally gets his bill of health from Dr. Twombly so he can graduate the fifth grade, but Twombly really wants to talk to Grannie again. Drysdale brings him to the Clampetts' just as Grannie showed Pearl how to use a love potion, so everyone thinks Twombly's interest in Grannie is love.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Get Psychoanalyzed" - Jethro needs a health certificate to graduate from the fifth grade, so he goes to the only doctor the Clampetts know about - Mrs. Drysdale's psychiatrist. He gets nowhere with Jethro, so Pearl comes and she thinks he is testing her virtue when he tries to lay her on his couch.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts in Court" - When a couple who ran into the Clampett's car discover that they're rich, they fake injuries and sue the Clampetts.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Entertain" - Jed arranges a party to get Grannie out of her doldrums and invites the Drysdale. But when Mr. Drysdale's boss comes and wants to meet them for dinner, he wonders if he's going to be sent to an Alaskan bank when his boss finds out he's been lying about Jed and his family being sophisticated.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Duke Becomes a Father" - Mlle. Denise comes back for the birth of her dog's puppies, and to see Jed. They do some "courtin' and sparkin'" Mrs. Drysdale isn't nearly so happy when she finds the puppies share more in common with Duke the bloodhound than Claude the poodle.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts and the Dodgers" - Jed and Jethro go golfing with Leo Durocher, coach of the Los Angeles Dodgers, who wants to recruit Jethro as a pitcher when he sees how well he can throw.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Pays His Income Tax" - When an IRS agent gets chased away by Granny, Mr. Drysdale tells him the story of how the Clampetts came to be rich and move to Beverly Hills.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Granny's Spring Tonic" - Miss Hathaway's brilliant-but-frumpy bank protegee Gloria is really a sultry gold-digger with eyes for Jed's $34 million, and Jed's just had his annual dose of Granny's spring tonic.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Cuts the Family Tree" - Mrs. Drysdale continues to be forced into doing "practices from the past" by Mrs. Smith-Standish, who discovers that Jed's family is possibly the first to come the country. If so they'd be famous worldwide, but does Jed want that?

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Family Tree" - Mrs. Drysdale wants to get rid of the Clampetts before the arrival of Mrs. Smith-Standish, the head of a 'first family' historical society. But Mrs. Smith-Standish goes to the Clampetts' first and becomes enchanted with their antiques and way of life, even to roping the horrified Mrs. Drysdale into helping do the chores the old-fashioned way.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Becomes a Banker" - Drysdale hires Jed to be a banker when he needs a crack shot to help beat a rival banker in a skeet shooting competition, but his partner needs to be an employee.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Buys the Freeway" - Henry Jones shows up at the Clampett's, pretends to be an old friend and tries to sell the family the Hollywood Bowl, Griffith Park, and the freeway.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Duke Steals a Wife" - Jed and Duke his dog are both down without a woman in their lives, until they see and pursue a French woman and her poodle that she brought to breed with Mrs. Drysdales dog. Meanwhile Mrs. Drysdale has arranged a "marriage" between the dogs, complete with a decorated bedroom.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Plays Solomon" - Granny can't stand Pearl's yodeling so she reports her to the police. When one of the officers takes Pearl out, Granny thinks she got Pearl in trouble and becomes sad and contrite. Meanwhile, the police find her still, and new trouble results.

programme.title: Bells of San Angelo
programme.desc: Roy Rogers and Dale Evans team up in this tale of silver mines, kidnappings, and secret passages. Roy plays a border guard who has to rescue Dale from the clutches of underhanded miner Rex Gridley (John McGuire). How does he do it? With help from Trigger, of course! With a cast that inclues Andy Devine as Sheriff Cookie Bullfincer, director William Witney gives us an enjoyable drama from the golden era of Republic Pictures!

programme.title: Home in Oklahoma
programme.desc: The entire gang is here! Roy Rogers, Dale Evans, Gabby Hayes, and Trigger are all involved in a murder mystery involving the death of a wealthy ranch owner. Songs include I Wish I Was A Kid Again and Cowboy Ham and Eggs.

programme.title: Way of the West
programme.desc: Government agent Gordon (Hal Taliaferro) goes undercover to investigate an ongoing war between cattlemen and sheepmen. But he soon finds himself framed for murder! This low budget western from the short-lived Empire Pictures is from the golden era of shoot em ups, when audiences knew who the bad guys were, and couldnt wait for the good guys to serve up some just punishment!

programme.title: Mohawk
programme.desc: Kurt Neumann (The Fly, Rocketship X-M) directed this bawdy tale of a love that was louder than war drums! With a cast that includes Scott Brady, Rita Gam, and Neville Brand, the story takes place on a remote army post where an artist romances a trio of women, just as a vengeful settler turns the army and the local Iroquois tribe against each other. Film buffs might like to know that the cinematography for this feature is by four-time Oscar nominee Karl Struss, who won an Academy Award in 1929 for his work on Sunrise.

programme.title: Doc Holliday
programme.desc: Doc Holliday runs a small dental practice in rural Georgia when he is visited by Wyatt Earp and offered a chance to become a United States Deputy. All Doc has to do is capture the Bridle Brothers - two of most notorious outlaws the West has ever seen. The Doc sets out on an adventure like none other to find these hardened criminals and ends up forging a friendship with Wyatt Earp that would carry into the events of Tombstone.

programme.title: Broken Arrow
programme.desc: For a decade, the white settlers and the Apaches have been engaged in a bloody war with no peaceful end in sight. When a white scout, Tom Jeffords (James Stewart), has a dangerously close but enlightening encounter with the natives, he begins to see the humanity in these enemies. Entering the Apache territory seeking peace, Jeffords forms a friendship with the Apache leader Cochise (Jeff Chandler), although there are people on both sides who resist extending the olive branch.

programme.title: European Poker Tour
programme.desc: Main Event Episode 1 (ME1)-After a three-year hiatus, the prestigious EPT Monte Carlo €5,000 Main Event returns in spectacular fashion. Day 2 brings together poker's biggest names, including high-stakes specialist Patrik Antonius, World Champion Koray Aldemir, Italian ace Mustapha Kanit, and trailblazing two-time EPT champion Victoria Coren-Mitchell. The battle for poker immortality begins.

programme.title: European Poker Tour
programme.desc: Main Event Episode 2 (ME2)-Tension mounts as the money bubble approaches in Monte Carlo's Main Event. With 159 players set to cash for at least €8,690, every decision matters. EPT legends Patrik Antonius and Davidi Kitai command the feature table while 2019 PSPC champion Ramon Colillas holds the chip lead. Who will survive the bubble and who will go home empty-handed?

programme.title: European Poker Tour
programme.desc: Main Event Episode 3 (ME3)-The money bubble has burst, but for seasoned pro Ole Schemion-who has earned over $5 million in Monte Carlo alone-a minimum cash isn't enough. He faces a formidable challenge from French star Gaëlle Baumann and online legend Jussi Nevanlinna at the feature table. With real money now guaranteed, the gloves come off.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Season 3 returns to the studio with a spring episode that puts handmade pasta front and center. Michael Symon makes his grandmother's Cavatellióa ricotta-based pasta dough (1 cup flour, 1 egg, 1.5 cups ricotta, Parmesan) that is more forgiving than traditional egg pasta, closer in spirit to a dumpling than a proper noodle. He demonstrates the technique his grandmother taught him as a child: roll the dough into thin strands, cut into inch-long pieces, and press-roll each one with a butter knife to create a hollow, ridged shape that opens slightly when cooked and catches every drop of sauce. Carla has a revelation mid-episode realizing cavatelli is essentially a dumplingóthe Italian version of a global tradition that spans every culture. The sauce is spring itself: brown butter carried almost to the edge, blanched peas, bias-cut asparagus, pasta water, and lemon. Michael also reflects on the memory of cooking as familyópasta making as a group project, a Sunday activity with kids or grandkids, something imperfect that matters anyway. The episode weaves in conversations about spring weather chaos on the East Coast, Gen X collectively aging past the Golden Girls cast, Michael's habit of texting Cleveland friends his Venice Beach weather reports, and a team-meeting story where Nick's extended hand produced entirely different reactions from all three hosts based on their respective cultural backgrounds. Nick debuts a new segment, 'Choose Wisely,' a spring-edition 'most likely to' game. Sponsored by Fresh Direct and Front Gate.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly leads this Chewed Up Bites episode by transforming leftover rotisserie chicken into a colorful, crunchy chicken salad inspired by the classic Chinese chicken salad popularized by Wolfgang Puck’s Chinois. As he assembles a simple sesame-lime vinaigrette and tosses together cabbage, carrots, bok choy, bell peppers, scallions, and shredded chicken, the hosts discuss the value of leftovers, pantry staples, and the difference between a salad and simply a bowl of lettuce. Michael Symon shares thoughts on menu development and simple comfort foods like peanut butter and jelly sandwiches. The conversation evolves into a broader discussion about healthy eating habits, meal planning, and the realization that not every meal needs to be an indulgent experience. Along the way, the hosts joke about Clinton’s “Gay Soup Diet,” debate the merits of themed ingredient weeks, and exchange playful banter about fashion makeovers, aging, and personal style. Sponsor shout-outs to FreshDirect and Frontgate are woven into the episode, highlighting convenient grocery delivery and entertaining essentials. A fan mail segment sparks more comedy, including the possibility of a future Gay Soup Diet cookbook. The episode wraps with the hosts sampling the finished salad, celebrating its fresh flavors and nutritional balance, before signing off with thanks to viewers and listeners.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon, Carla Hall, and Clinton Kelly welcome acclaimed Miami chef Michelle Bernstein to a special Miami Beach episode of Chewed Up filmed at the W Hotel in partnership with Experience Miami Beach. After sampling local bakery favorites including guava pastries and tequeños, the hosts celebrate Miami’s vibrant culinary scene and discuss Michelle’s lasting impact on South Florida food culture as she shares stories about her career, mentorship, and being honored during the South Beach Wine & Food Festival’s 25th anniversary celebration. Michelle then teaches the group how to make her signature Cuban sandwich empanadas, transforming the classic Miami staple into a crispy handheld pastry filled with lechón, ham, cheese, pickles, and yellow mustard. As they assemble and fry their empanadas, the conversation ranges from Cuban sandwich authenticity and Latin food traditions to family roots and restaurant life. The group also plays a lively round of “Chew It or Screw It,” debating topics such as late dinner reservations, over-tipping, flip-flops at fine dining restaurants, poolside rosé, and afternoon Cuban coffee. During Fan Mail, they answer viewer questions about cooking with candied jalapeños, discuss which decade they would most like to have grown up in, and share their favorite ways to unwind after a long day. The episode wraps with the hosts tasting Michelle’s empanadas, praising the crisp dough and balanced flavors, while Michelle shares details about her new restaurant, La Cañita. Two audience giveaways are awarded before the hosts encourage viewers to experience everything Miami Beach has to offer.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Top Chef alum and Carla Hall's culinary producer Ariane Duarte joins the Chewed Up crew to make spring crostini three ways on homemade sourdough focaccia, while the hosts debate food rules and play Chew It or Screw It.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall makes a vibrant frozen pea soup in under 20 minutes—sautéed onions, stock, mint, parsley, white pepper, and cream blended silky smooth—then leads the hosts in Peep Roulette, a blind flavor-guessing game. Clinton wins; Michael is traumatized.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: In this “Munch Madness” episode of Chewed Up, Carla Hall, Clinton Kelly, and Michael Symon put eight frozen pizzas through a bracket-style taste test inspired by March Madness. The hosts compare each pairing, breaking down crust, sauce, cheese, and overall flavor while mixing in humor, nostalgia, and brutally honest reactions. As they move through the rounds, the conversation is fueled by strong opinions, unexpected preferences, and plenty of debate about what actually makes a good pizza. Michael’s love of authentic pizza drives passionate critiques, Clinton reflects on his long hiatus from frozen options, and Carla keeps things grounded in everyday practicality for busy households. The competition builds toward a final showdown that sparks lively discussion and a few surprising takes. The episode then shifts into a conversation about potlucks, outdoor dining, and picnics, blending personal preferences with playful arguments. It wraps with a fan giveaway and the hosts’ signature banter.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon revisits one of the most important dishes of his career as he makes his famous rotisserie chicken mac and cheese, a creamy pasta recipe that first became a hit at his Cleveland restaurant Piccolo Mondo in 1992. Using rigatoni, heavy cream, goat cheese, fresh rosemary, and shredded rotisserie chicken, Michael walks Clinton Kelly and Carla Hall through the deceptively simple recipe while sharing stories from his early days as a 22-year-old executive chef running a packed live-fire kitchen. The conversation turns nostalgic as Michael explains how the dish followed him from restaurant to restaurant and became such a customer favorite that he eventually had to remove it from the menu. The hosts laugh over Michael’s son Kyle unknowingly eating goat cheese for years despite insisting he hated it, while Clinton passionately defends rosemary after recalling a social media argument about the herb. The episode also features sponsor shout-outs to Fresh Direct and Frontgate, plus playful banter about portion sizes, “fat soup diets,” and airplane etiquette after eating rich pasta dishes. Once plated, the hosts rave about the comforting flavors, especially how the chicken melts into the creamy sauce and how the rosemary balances the tanginess of the goat cheese. The episode wraps with the selection of Melicia from New York as the “Fan of the Day,” followed by more joking between the hosts as they finish off the rich and nostalgic dish.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Hometown Week brings Michael Symon's roots to the Chewed Up kitchen, as he makes his grandfather Pap's potato and farmer's cheese pierogis—a dish that traces back to the heavy Eastern European influence in both Cleveland, where Michael was born, and Pittsburgh, where his parents grew up. The dough is Pap's recipe: all-purpose flour, a pinch of salt, one egg, sour cream for tenderness, and softened butter worked in for extra fat and pliability—a forgiving dough that rests in the fridge for an hour before being rolled and cut into rounds. The filling is pure simplicity: Idaho potato riced and folded with farmer's cheese in a 2:1 ratio, seasoned with chives, salt, and pepper. Michael boils the pierogis until they float, then finishes them in a pan with caramelized onions, butter, a splash of pasta water, and fresh chives—garnished tableside with sour cream. The episode's centerpiece story is the birth of Lola's famous beef cheek pierogi: when it first went on the Cleveland menu in 1997, diners balked at 'beef cheek,' and Michael sold only one or two a night. He renamed it 'pot roast pierogi,' sold 70 the next night, ran it for a year until diners were hooked, then quietly switched the name back to beef cheek. It became the restaurant's most popular appetizer by a factor of ten. Carla mentions that her nephew AJ is addicted to Michael's GoldBelly pierogis—available in potato and cheese, pork and sauerkraut, and beef cheek. The episode features three debate topics: leftovers vs. the original meal (unanimous chew it, with the exception of reheated steak and fish); the simplest dishes being the hardest to execute (cacio e pepe, French omelet, pie crust, biscuits, vanilla ice cream); and breakfast for dinner (universal love, best kept occasional—or as a late-night eggs tradition after a couple of old fashioneds). The game is Michael's Two Truths and a Lie: he slipped on butter and dropped a full tray at Lola (true); he set off a fire alarm while cooking at a friend's house (true); and he once cooked pierogis on a George Foreman Grill and melted the plastic drip tray (the lie—Clinton guesses correctly, consistent with Michael's famous contempt for George Foremans, air fryers, and microwaves). Fan mail covers: favorite cake (Clinton: carrot cake with raisins; Michael: coconut; Carla: German chocolate); what to do with canned crab (Michael: fold it into a pierogi filling or make angry crab pasta; Carla: red sauce pasta with crab); and why chefs hate tilapia (muddy flavor, overcrowded farming, general bad juju). The fan giveaway offers a monogrammed Michael Symon apron from his Frontgate collection and a copy of Simply Symon Suppers—won by Sue from Columbus, Georgia. Sponsored by FreshDirect and Front Gate.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly takes center stage for “Home Week” as he shares a deeply personal dish from his Long Island upbringing: spaghetti with white clam sauce. Drawing on childhood memories of digging for clams in the mudflats of Port Jefferson Station, Clinton walks through a simple yet flavorful recipe using fresh little neck clams, garlic-infused olive oil, white wine, and parsley. As he cooks, the hosts—Carla Hall and Michael Symon—swap stories about growing up, working in restaurants, and stepping outside their comfort zones, with Clinton reflecting on his journey from introversion to becoming a television personality. The episode balances cooking with lively debate, including a spirited discussion about oversized restaurant menus and the etiquette of bringing your own hot sauce to a meal. Michael delivers a passionate rant about seasoning, while Carla offers thoughtful insights on culinary authenticity. The trio’s chemistry shines through playful teasing and shared appreciation for food that tells a story. As the pasta comes together, Clinton emphasizes technique—like gently infusing garlic at low heat and using reserved clam juice for depth of flavor—resulting in a dish that is both accessible and impressive. The episode wraps with the hosts tasting the final product, praising its balance and simplicity. A fun game segment, “Clinton or Clam,” adds humor, followed by fan mail and a giveaway announcement featuring a signed cookbook. The show closes on a warm, communal note, celebrating food, friendship, and the stories behind every dish.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall takes center stage during “Home Week” as she introduces Michael Symon and Clinton Kelly to a nostalgic Nashville staple: the hot fish sandwich. Drawing from her childhood memories, Carla explains that unlike the city’s famous hot chicken, this dish is all about being served piping hot straight from the oil rather than spicy. As she prepares cornmeal-crusted catfish, she shares stories of visiting local spots like Ed’s Fish, where the sandwich was a simple, affordable treat enjoyed on Fridays and often eaten outdoors. The hosts dive into a lively conversation about how Nashville has transformed over the years, balancing its old-school charm with its modern “Nash Vegas” energy, while also reflecting on how food traditions connect to community and shared experiences. Carla walks through the cooking process step-by-step, offering practical tips on frying, seasoning, and building the perfect sandwich with white bread, yellow mustard, hot sauce, pickles, and onions. Once assembled, the sandwich sparks an enthusiastic reaction, with the hosts praising its comforting, craveable simplicity. The conversation expands into broader themes about slowing down, engaging with others, and the lost art of waiting for food. The episode continues with fan mail, where the hosts share personal stories about Easter traditions, hidden talents, and the nuances of giving honest feedback. A playful hometown trivia game rounds things out, followed by a humorous and eclectic giveaway from Carla’s own closet. The episode closes on a high note, celebrating the joy of food, memory, and connection

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon welcome Angie Rito and Scott Tacinelli, the chef-owners behind the celebrated New York restaurant Don Angie, for a lively episode dedicated to Italian American comfort food. The conversation begins with admiration for the restaurant’s iconic lasagna before shifting to the main event: a deeply flavorful, “life-changing” meatball recipe inspired by a blend of both chefs’ grandmothers’ traditions. As Angie and Scott walk through their process, they share chef-driven techniques like using a milk-soaked panade for moisture, puréed onions for even flavor distribution, and roasted garlic to avoid harsh raw bites. The hosts jump in with questions and personal anecdotes, sparking a broader discussion about Italian versus Italian American cooking, rule-breaking in the kitchen, and regional food identity—from Cleveland to New York. The group also plays a spirited round of “Discuss,” debating topics like garlic usage, cheese on seafood, and proper spaghetti etiquette, leading to humorous and opinionated exchanges. Along the way, they highlight the philosophy behind Don Angie’s food: bold, unapologetic flavors built on cheese, garlic, and tradition—without strict adherence to old-world rules. As the meatballs finish baking, they’re tossed in a simple, bright tomato sauce and served family-style, earning rave reviews from the hosts for their rich texture and layered flavor. The episode wraps with a fan giveaway of the chefs’ cookbook and a final celebration of Italian American cuisine, emphasizing creativity, collaboration, and the joy of cooking without limits.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon take on the ultimate comfort food by each creating their own version of a grilled cheese sandwich, showcasing three distinct styles that range from classic to elevated. Michael leads with a rich and decadent Croque Madame, complete with a homemade mornay sauce, gruyère, ham, and a perfectly cooked egg on top, sharing tips on building flavor and texture through technique. Carla follows with a Southern-inspired twist, hollowing out a brioche bun to stuff it with pimento cheese and tangy chow chow pickles, emphasizing balance and texture in every bite. Clinton rounds things out with a fan-inspired take on a classic grilled cheese, combining American, cheddar, and gruyère cheeses on rye bread, using mayonnaise for an evenly crisp, golden exterior. Throughout the episode, the trio trades stories, jokes, and cooking insights, debating everything from sandwich ratios to restaurant culture. They later shift into a lively “overrated vs. underrated” game, diving into food trends, brunch culture, and even aging, blending humor with candid reflections. The episode wraps with a fan giveaway and appreciation for sponsors, leaving viewers inspired to experiment with their own grilled cheese creations at home.

programme.desc: Mary Ann teaches her Scuola Culinaria students how to make a beyond-moist-and-tender Apricot Yogurt Pound Cake. programme.title: Ciao Italia
programme.desc: Mary Ann reveals the secret to Crepes in Chicken Broth: make the savory broth from scratch with fresh ingredients and elevates the very versatile crepe to new heights. Then...never heard of Tomato Marmelade? Mary Ann show her students how tomatoes, grated ginger, a clove-dotted onion, sugar, and—surprise!—balsamic vinegar—make a terrific topping for fresh ricotta, bruschetta, or cheese.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: Begin Season 2 with a refresher course on the basic smoking techniques and smokers, from rotisserie-smoking chicken to smoke-roasting stuffed pork loin. Learn how to smoke on a kettle grill, pellet smoker, electric smoker, and offset barrel smoker.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: Some of the world's greatest poultry dishes owe their soulful character to wood smoke. Find out how to master the fundamentals of smoking poultry—from curing and brining to smoking in charcoal smokers and pellet grills. Plus, discover how to electrify barbecue sauce with chipotle (smoked jalapeño) chilies.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: This show explores a region you don't normally associate with smoking: Asia. From Chinese char siu pork to smoky tangerine flan, learn to create a unique fusion of Asian barbecue and American wood smoke.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: Once you get the hang of smoking, you can smoke just about everything. Discover how to use electric smokers, pellet grills, ceramic cookers, and stovetop kettle smokers to add soulful smoke flavors to a spectacular four-course meal.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: Find out how to apply classic ham curing and smoking techniques to anything, from spareribs to turkey legs, to create dishes that are anything but traditional. Plus, learn to make ham in a hurry and an eye-popping bacon dessert.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: Wake up and smell the wood smoke. Some of the world's best breakfast dishes—from bacon to hash—depend on the irresistible flavor of smoke. Discover four smoky twists on breakfast that will give you a reason to rise and shine.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Smoke
programme.desc: Usually, smoking requires time and patience. But what if you could achieve great smoke flavor in 5 to 10 minutes? From steak to cocktails, learn how to smoke in a snap.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Diane discovers the history of her historic neighborhood in the heart of central Athens with the city's most renowned urban chronicler, and cooks up a delicious Spiced Roasted Chicken with Toasted Orzo Pilaf, Kale Caesar with Greek yogurt and a Crispy Phyllo Ice Cream Sundae with Honey, Nuts and Halva.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Diane's artist daughter, Kyveli, invites a few gallerists and young artists for dinner in her studio in Exarcheia, a fascinating Athens neighborhood, and Diane cooks up the perfect meal to inspire their insightful conversation. On the menu: Braised Ouzo-Orange Octopus, Zucchini-Herb Phyllo Pie, and a Giant Bean Greek Salad.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Diane and Chef Lefteris Lazarou, often called the father of modern Greek cuisine, explore the fish market and Diane gets a few lessons on how to choose the best seafood. At his restaurant in Piraeus, Chef prepares a Pan-Seared Grouper and Whole Squid with Spanakopita Puree. Diane, inspired by Chef, creates seafood-stuffed cabbage rolls in her home.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Diane prepares a dinner party meal for a handful of her closest friends. Diane provides helpful tips to alleviate the stress that comes with planning a dinner party and provides easy recipes that are healthy and delicious. On the menu: Char-Broiled Cabbage "Steaks" with Pomegranate Seeds and Lemon Zest, Whole Baked Fish with Leeks, and Greek Yogurt Panna Cotta.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Greece is a cornucopia of age-old natural superfoods. Diane invites a well-known dietician and together they prepare a healthy meal brimming with Greek superfoods including a Spiced Carrot Soup Three Ways, a Black-Eyed Pea Salad with Dried Figs, Pomegranate, EVOO and Herbs; and a dessert of wheat kernels, Greek honey, nuts, and dried fruits called Varvara.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: With Athens as a backdrop, Diane explores the foods that so many of us have in common regardless of where we are from. Can soba noodles be grecophied? Can a classic spanakopita be a filling for an American baked potato? Diane answers these questions and ventures beyond tradition into the global world, embracing new ingredients, and christens new recipes.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Tony returns to his beloved Hanoi, Vietnam to visit his friend, Linh, for the Vietnamese lunar New Year holiday of Tet. Together they enjoy the holiday and indulge in all the exotic delicacies Vietnamese culture offers.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Mekong River, Vietnam: On the river, Tony drinks a lot of Mekong moonshine with the welcoming owners of a duck farm. Although seduced by his Vietnam experience, Tony is not sure the royal dish Birds Nest Soup is all its cracked up to be.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Tony sets out to find the real Singapore, with its multicultural restaurants and food markets. He samples prescription food dishes, explores the Geyland Serai Market, and rides the clean and efficient local subway.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Melbourne, Australia boasts hard-living, good-natured, and eccentric lovers of food - Tony's kind of people. In search of the outlaws of food, Tony explores a pasteurization-free cheese factory, and an old-world cured jamon workshop.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam: After taking a cyclo-ride through Ho Chi Minh City and trying various foods from roadside vendors and markets, Tony tests his might with a few shots of snake wine and a live cobra heart.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Napa, CA: Marooned in San Francisco, Tony tastes local shellfish and durian ice cream before embarking on a pilgrimage to experience the genius of Thomas Keller, chef and owner of The French Laundry.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Known as Brazil's capital of happiness, Salvador da Bahia offers fruitful local markets, and rich Bahian cuisine. Here, Tony takes part in a traditional local ceremony, and experiences the culinary talents of the famous Bahian artist of the kitchen - Chef Dada.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: New York, NY and Provincetown, MA: Experience the formative moments of Tony's career as he revisits his first job, the culinary institute that taught him the basics, a normal workday at Les Halles Brasserie, and his mysterious mentor, Bigfoot.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: St. Petersburg, Russia: Tony plays spy in Russia and sets off to locate the best blinis and borscht St. Petersburg has to offer.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Tony travels to the grain-belt capital of Minneapolis, Minnesota to experience firsthand the conflict between creative independent operators and generic franchises.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Portugal & Southwest France: Visiting his boss's family farm, Tony helps slaughter and feast on a pig the family has been fattening all year. Then, Tony heads to France to experience the rich delights of a foie gras farm.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Los Angeles, CA: New York chef, Anthony Bourdain, descends into the culinary underbelly of his metropolitan nemesis, Los Angeles, where he experiences the trials of celebrity fame and the joys of West Coast cuisine.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: A spontaneous layover in Bangkok, Thailand leads Tony into the city, where he tries dishes of deep-fried frog skins and pla rah, a fish-paste that has fermented anywhere from three months to a year.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Tony embarks on a pilgrimage to Sydney, Australia so he may indulge in a meal made by one of the world's best chefs - Tetsuya Wakuda.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: Cambodia: With a friend along for the ride, Tony travels to Phnom Penh, Cambodia, where he explores a food culture that is both pleasing to the palette and slightly frightening.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain A Cook's Tour
programme.desc: New York, NY: Passionate about his city, Tony gives us an intimate tour of the New York City foods he loves - sturgeon and bagels, fluke ceviche, pan-seared foie gras, braised veal cheek, authentic cheese and, of course, a hotdog.
